element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Underpowered at start; weak gun, slow speed. Players learn basic movement and shooting against weakest enemies
Power meter system allows players to select desired upgrades from menu (Speed-Up, Shield, Missile, Laser, Option). Single item type advances menu position
Requires understanding power-up menu selection and timing. Team tested 20+ movement patterns; final design provides orbital support fire
Players learn to manage power meter and select appropriate ordnance for enemy types and situations
Provides defensive capability; requires meter management and strategic selection

element_introduction_order.element: Boss encounters (giant craft with exposed cores)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Iconic 'Destroy the core!' mechanic. Bosses feature straight passage to blue core(s), with destructible walls blocking access and weapon emplacements creating avoidance patterns. Cores change blue→red when critical
Designer Hiroyasu prioritized visually distinct worlds with unique enemies and locations—relatively uncommon for shooters at time of release
Alternative boss design; weak spots in mouth, head, or eye rather than blue cores. Some bosses regenerate wall structures

typical_relief_triggers: Boss core destruction and color change (blue to red)
typical_tension_triggers: Weapon emplacement patterns from giant craft bodies
Destructible wall barriers blocking core access
Wave progression with increasingly difficult enemy patterns
Loss of powered-up state (e.g., shield depletion)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Horizontally scrolling continuous assault with escalating enemy complexity. Power meter management provides player-driven pacing control. Boss battles feature sustained tension broken by incremental core damage progress

difficulty_curve.early_game: Weak Vic Viper with basic gun introduces vulnerability. Players face single-hit weak enemies while learning power meter system and establishing power-up priority (Speed-Up typically needed first)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Powered-up arsenal enables engagement with varied enemy types across distinct environments. Increased wave complexity and enemy attack patterns
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained high-intensity combat requiring optimal power-up configuration. Boss encounters demand precision shooting through defensive wall structures while managing avoidance patterns
notable_spikes: First boss encounter (Stage 3 Moai) - introduces core destruction mechanic and sustained defensive fire patterns
Organic boss variants - require different weak-spot targeting than core-based designs

notable_level_patterns.description: Giant craft bosses feature one or more blue-colored spheres (cores) at center, accessible via straight passage. Destructible walls impede bullet access; weapon emplacements on craft body create avoidance requirements. Core changes blue→red at critical condition. Some bosses regenerate walls
Non-mechanical bosses with weak spots in anatomical locations (mouth, head, eye) rather than exposed cores. Requires targeting precision and pattern avoidance
Player advancement gated by power meter management. Must collect capsules, advance menu, and select desired upgrades. Speed-Up typically required early; weapon selection scales with enemy complexity
